clickMetricHint: Click any metric for a deeper analysis
concentrationHigh: Your top category accounts for {pct}% of revenue. Consider diversifying to reduce dependency.
concentrationModerate: Top category is {pct}% of revenue. Room to improve mix diversity.
concentrationLow: Good mix diversity — top category is only {pct}% of revenue.
noCategoryData: No category data available yet. Tag your products with categories to see the mix breakdown.
kpiDailyAvgTip1: Your daily average factors in slow and busy days
kpiDailyAvgTip2: Use this as a baseline to measure promotional impact
kpiDailyAvgTip3: Track this weekly — a rising average signals healthy growth
kpiDailyAvgTip4: If flat or declining, check foot traffic and marketing reach
kpiDailyAvgTipNo1: Start recording sales to establish a baseline
kpiDailyAvgTipNo2: Consistent daily tracking unlocks trend analysis
kpiDailyAvgActionYes: Set a daily target 10% above your current average and track progress
kpiDailyAvgActionNo: Record at least 7 days of sales data to establish a meaningful average
kpiDailyAvgAskPrompt: Analyse my daily sales average — is it trending up or down? What days are pulling the average down and what can I do about it?
kpiTodayVsAvgBenchmarkAbove: Above average — great day so far
kpiTodayVsAvgTipAbove1: Today is outperforming your weekly average
kpiTodayVsAvgTipAbove2: Check what is driving the uplift — promotion, day of week, weather?
kpiTodayVsAvgTipAbove3: Replicate winning conditions on slower days
kpiTodayVsAvgTipBelow1: Today is tracking below your average
kpiTodayVsAvgTipBelow2: Check: Is it early in the day? The pace may pick up
kpiTodayVsAvgTipBelow3: Consider a flash promotion or social media push to drive traffic
kpiTodayVsAvgTipBelow4: Compare with the same day last week for context
kpiTodayVsAvgActionAbove: Document what made today successful so you can replicate it
kpiTodayVsAvgActionBelow: Consider a limited-time offer to boost sales before end of day
kpiGrossMarginBenchmarkBelow: Below average — review pricing and costs
kpiGrossMarginBenchmarkCritical: Critical — margins too thin to sustain
kpiGrossMarginTipGood1: Margins are healthy — protect your pricing power
kpiGrossMarginTipGood2: Look for premium upsell opportunities to widen margins further
kpiGrossMarginTipGood3: Monitor supplier costs for any creeping increases
kpiGrossMarginTipGood4: Consider whether volume growth could dilute margins
kpiGrossMarginTipWarn1: Margins are tight — review your top cost items
kpiGrossMarginTipWarn2: Negotiate better rates with your top 3 suppliers
kpiGrossMarginTipWarn3: Consider bundling low-margin items with high-margin ones
kpiGrossMarginTipWarn4: Check if any products are selling below cost
kpiGrossMarginTipCrit1: Margins are critically low — urgent action needed
kpiGrossMarginTipCrit3: Consider discontinuing items with negative margins
kpiGrossMarginTipCrit4: Raise prices where the market will bear it — even 5% helps
kpiGrossMarginActionGood: Focus on maintaining margins while growing volume — avoid unnecessary discounting
kpiGrossMarginActionBad: Urgently review your pricing and cost structure this week
kpiAvgBasketTip1: Average basket size indicates customer spending per visit
kpiAvgBasketTip2: Upselling and cross-selling can increase basket value by 15-30%
kpiAvgBasketTip3: Bundle offers and combo deals are proven basket builders
kpiAvgBasketTip4: Staff training on suggestive selling has the highest ROI
kpiAvgBasketTip5: Track basket size by time of day — lunch vs dinner baskets often differ significantly
kpiAvgBasketTipNo: Start tracking to see your average transaction value
kpiAvgBasketActionYes: Introduce a "frequently bought together" suggestion or create a combo deal to boost basket size
kpiAvgBasketActionNo: Record sales to start tracking basket sizes
kpiNetSalesTip1: Net sales = gross revenue minus refunds and discounts
kpiNetSalesTipNoRefunds: No refunds today — great quality control
kpiNetSalesTipDiscounts: Discounts: {value} — ensure these are driving volume
kpiNetSalesTip4: High refund rates often signal product quality issues or mismatched expectations
kpiNetSalesTip5: Track your discount-to-revenue ratio — over 10% may indicate pricing problems
kpiNetSalesActionRefunds: Investigate the root cause of refunds — is it product quality, wrong items, or buyer remorse?
kpiNetSalesActionClean: Keep refund rates low by maintaining quality and clear product descriptions
kpiPeakHourTip1: Your busiest trading hour — ensure full staffing during this window
kpiPeakHourTip2: Pre-prepare stock and supplies before peak to avoid bottlenecks
kpiPeakHourTip3: Consider premium pricing or time-limited offers during peak
kpiPeakHourTip4: Track peak hour changes by day of week — weekday vs weekend peaks differ
kpiPeakHourTip5: Off-peak hours are opportunities for promotions to flatten demand
kpiPeakHourTipNo: Record more sales data to identify your peak trading hour
kpiPeakHourActionYes: Ensure you have maximum staff coverage during peak hour and pre-stage your most popular items
kpiPeakHourActionNo: Track sales by hour for at least a week to identify patterns
chartThisWeekVsPrev: This Week Daily — vs Previous Week Average
askPromptTime: Analyse my sales time patterns — which hours and days are performing best? Where should I focus staffing?
askPromptCategories: Analyse my product category mix — am I too dependent on one category? What should I promote or diversify into?
askPromptPayments: Analyse my payment methods and staff performance — who are my top performers and how can I improve the team?
askPromptDefault: Analyse my POS sales data in detail
